mechanism_narrative: The prevailing level of the federal funds rate directly determines the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows; a higher FEDFUNDS level mechanically raises discount rates and reduces present values, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: A two-year rolling Rank IC between FEDFUNDS levels and 20-day forward returns is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its historical median.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.5% signals slack in the labor market that precedes downward revisions to corporate revenue and margin expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean forward return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains static or rises more slowly, the resulting decline in real policy rates stimulates borrowing and asset reallocation into equities; this joint dynamic produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL momentum and FEDFUNDS levels fails to produce a positive and significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns, or the joint signal underperforms a naive long-only benchmark over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in housing starts indicate that interest-sensitive demand is rolling over, which foreshadows broader consumption and investment contraction and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in late-cycle periods (defined by simultaneous low UNRATE and above-median FEDFUNDS), or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects positive supply-side momentum and capacity utilization improvements that translate into near-term earnings surprises, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: A rolling 2-year Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of months with accelerating INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that directly pressures top-line revenue growth for consumer-facing equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean forward return during decline months is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates durable income formation and household spending capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 60-day returns during post-recession recovery periods (defined as 12-36 months after a business cycle trough), or the hit rate of positive returns falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures the aggregate output trajectory of the economy; positive GDPC1 momentum confirms demand expansion that supports corporate profit growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to exhibit a positive and significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of quarters with accelerating GDPC1.
